tutorial.title: Binary Search
tutorial.explanation: Binary Search works on sorted answers or sorted arrays by repeatedly checking the middle position.
tutorial.practiceTip: Write the low, high, and mid update rules carefully on paper before coding.

overviewParagraphs: Binary search halves the search space every step when the answer range is monotonic.
In competitive programming this appears both on sorted arrays and on answer space problems where a predicate flips from false to true.

tutorial.title: DFS
tutorial.explanation: Depth-First Search explores as far as possible before backtracking in a graph or tree.
tutorial.practiceTip: Practice marking visited nodes first so you do not repeat work.

overviewParagraphs: DFS walks one branch deeply before backtracking, which makes it a core tool for trees, graphs, and recursive state exploration.
It is especially useful for connected components, subtree logic, and reachability.
